subject: Databases
title: Entity-Relationship Modeling
description: A quick win: understand Entity-Relationship Modeling.

hook.text: Before writing CREATE TABLE, draw the entities and their relationships. An ER diagram is the blueprint.

buildup.text: Entities are nouns: User, Order, Product. Attributes are properties: name, price, quantity.

discovery.text: Relationships connect entities: a User places an Order. An Order contains Products.

twist.text: The diagram exposes design problems early. Missing a relationship on paper is cheap. Missing it in code is expensive.

climax.text: Tools like dbdiagram.io and Lucidchart make ER diagrams fast to create and easy to share.

punchline.text: Draw the diagram first. Code the schema second.

quiz.question: Why should you create an ER diagram before writing SQL?
options: It reveals missing relationships and design flaws before they become expensive code problems
Databases require an ER diagram file to function
ER diagrams automatically generate the SQL for you
